summary.intent: Address a Greptile P1 security review comment on PR #627 by hardening the dcg install script in the dcg-guard hook example.
summary.outcome: Pinned dcg to tag v0.4.0, added SHA-256 checksum verification, removed the cache-busting date trick, updated HOOK.md documentation, and pushed commit 84c9988a to fortuna/moltis:patch-1.

repo: dcg-guard lives under examples/hooks/dcg-guard/ and has a HOOK.md companion doc that must stay in sync with install script changes.
PR #627 is on a fork branch (fortuna/moltis:patch-1), so pushes go to the fork rather than the upstream remote.

code.finding: HOOK.md was edited to reflect the pinned v0.4.0 tag, SHA-256 checksum verification step, removal of $(date +%s) cache-buster, and an optional review-before-run snippet.

workflow: Greptile must be explicitly re-triggered after a new commit; it does not auto-run on every push — polling was required to confirm a fresh review was queued.
Pinning to a git tag plus checksum verification is the expected standard for install-script security in this project.
Providing an alternative 'review-before-run' snippet alongside the one-liner is considered good practice for user trust.

friction: Had to poll Greptile multiple times to confirm it picked up the new HEAD and produced a fresh review.
YubiKey tap required for push, adding a manual step to the push workflow.
open_items: Verify Greptile clears the P1 finding after reviewing the updated HOOK.md and install script.
PR #627 still needs final approval and merge after Greptile sign-off.
